emails.body:
NVIDIA Developer Newsletter — Daily Digest #1
October 2, 2026

Top stories this week:

• CUDA 12.6 Release Candidate now available — includes new tensor memory accelerator (TMA) APIs for Hopper and Blackwell architectures, plus improved graph capture for dynamic workloads.
• TensorRT 10.2 adds native support for FP8 quantization pipelines and updated ONNX parser with opset 21 coverage.
• New cuDNN 9.1 preview brings grouped convolution optimizations for attention-heavy models.
• Developer blog: "Profiling Multi-GPU Training with NCU Log Analysis" — walkthrough of identifying communication bottlenecks in distributed LLM training.
• Upcoming webinar: "Blackwell Architecture Deep Dive" — Oct 15, 10:00 AM PT. Register at developer.nvidia.com/events.

Quick links:
- CUDA 12.6 RC download
- TensorRT 10.2 release notes
- cuDNN 9.1 preview access form
- NCU log analysis tutorial

NVIDIA Developer Newsletter — Daily Digest #2
October 3, 2026

In this edition:

• Nsight Compute 2026.3 released — new kernel timeline view with CPU-GPU correlation, plus automated optimization recommendations for memory-bound kernels.
• CUDA Python 12.6 wheels now on PyPI — includes CuPy-compatible array interface and experimental graph capture API.
• Triton 3.0 beta: just-in-time compilation for Hopper tensor cores, with new autotuner for persistent thread blocks.
• Case study: "Accelerating Weather Forecasting with Physics-Informed Neural Operators" — 40x speedup on H100 clusters using custom CUDA kernels.
• Security advisory: CVE-2026-3421 addressed in driver branch 560.35.03 — privilege escalation in UVM subsystem. Update recommended.

Resources:
- Nsight Compute 2026.3 download
- CUDA Python 12.6 install guide
- Triton 3.0 beta signup
- Weather forecasting technical blog
- Driver security bulletin

NVIDIA Developer Newsletter — Daily Digest #3
October 4, 2026

Highlights:

• cuOpt 2.1 adds multi-depot vehicle routing with time windows and stochastic demand modeling — targeted at logistics and supply chain optimization.
• New Modulus Sym 2.0 physics-ML framework release: differentiable physics solvers, improved PDE residual scaling, and distributed training support for digital twin workloads.
• RAPIDS 24.10: cuDF pandas accelerator now supports 98% of pandas API surface; cuML adds incremental PCA and HDBSCAN for out-of-core clustering.
• Developer spotlight: "Building Real-Time Digital Twins for Manufacturing" — interview with Siemens team using Omniverse + Modulus for predictive maintenance.
• GTC 2027 call for proposals opens Nov 1 — tracks include generative AI, simulation, robotics, and accelerated computing.

Links:
- cuOpt 2.1 release notes
- Modulus Sym 2.0 documentation
- RAPIDS 24.10 changelog
- Siemens digital twin interview
- GTC 2027 CFP details
